LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO TRAVEL ASSOCIATION DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR AS DUPLICATE ENTRY. =302/MJM
LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO TRAVEL ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT CONTINUED FROM OCT-21-2013 TO OCT-22-2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO TRAVEL ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE UNOPPOSED REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CAL. EV. CODE 452(C). THE ONLY FACTS ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT TO SUPPORT THREATENED UNLAWFUL BUSINESS PRACTICES AND TRADEMARK INFRINGEMENT ARE THAT DEFENDANT FILED APPLICATIONS WITH THE US PATENT AND TRADEMARK OFFICE AND FILED A REQUEST FOR AN EXTENSION OF TIME. THE COURT HAS JUDICIALLY NOTICED THE FACT THAT THE TRADEMARK APPLICATIONS HAVE BEEN EXPRESSLY ABANDONED. PLAINTIFF HAS 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE, IF HE CAN IN GOOD FAITH, ADDITIONAL FACTS TO SUPPORT THE THREATENED UNLAWFUL BUSINESS PRACTICES AND TRADEMARK INFRINGEMENT. ONE ORDER SIGNED ON THE DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN CSR #6185 (302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO TRAVEL ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S PRAYER FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS DENIED AS MOOT, IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE DEMURRER. ONE ORDER SIGNED ON THE DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN CSR #6185 (302/MJM)
MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-22-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-07-2014 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-01-2013 Continued To Jan-22-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-22-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO TRAVEL ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The unopposed Request for Judicial Notice is granted pursuant to Cal. Ev. Code 452(c). The only facts alleged in the Complaint to support threatened unlawful business practices and trademark infringement are that Defendant filed applications with the US Patent and Trademark Office and filed a request for an extension of time. The Court has judicially noticed the fact that the trademark applications have been expressly abandoned. Plaintiff has 20 days leave to amend to allege, if he can in good faith, additional facts to support the threatened unlawful business practices and trademark infringement. One order signed on the demurrer and motion to strike. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR #6185 (302/MJM)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-22-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO TRAVEL ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S PRAYER FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Denied as moot, in light of the ruling on the demurrer. One order signed on the demurrer and motion to strike. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR #6185 (302/MJM)
